shots.action: Establishing: dawn light over warehouse aisle seven, racks towering, floor tape faded.
Worker Dana clips on a hi-vis vest, slides in earbuds, and steps out of the break room.
A forklift reverses toward the blind corner; the convex mirror overhead is fogged and ignored.
shots.vo_line: The mirror was installed after the last one.

shots.action: The near-miss: the forklift emerges from the blind corner as Dana, earbuds in, steps off the pedestrian lane into its path.
The operator slams the brake; the pallet load shifts and shudders; the forks stop a hand's width from Dana.
shots.vo_line: A hand's width. That was the margin.

shots.action: Later: the supervisor types the incident report, the empty aisle behind the office glass.
shots.vo_line: Filed as paperwork. Near-miss number three.

shots.action: Replay from above: the same three seconds, seen from the convex mirror - both paths visible the whole time.
shots.vo_line: The mirror saw it all. Nobody looked.

shots.action: Corrective: Dana pockets the earbuds - earbuds out per the floor sign - walks the marked pedestrian lane, and the operator wipes the convex mirror clean.
shots.vo_line: Three fixes. Ten seconds. Nobody in the aisle guesses.
